pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe and it just hits different tonight. like, everything we do, every single emotion or struggle, it’s all just going to be zero. it’s all just noise that eventually gets silenced forever. it makes everything feel so pointless. why even bother trying to hold it all together when the math says it doesn't matter in the end?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm typing this. i just... i'm so tired. not like regular sleep tired, but like, my soul is just done. the lab results came back today and it's over. the funding is gone, the advisor won't even look me in the eye, and i realized i've spent the last 6 years building a life around a version of me that doesn't even exist anymore. i have nothing left. i'm just so exhausted from trying to keep my head above water. i just want everything to stop. i just want some kind of peace that doesn't involve waking up tomorrow.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very spark of my soul. He is gone, and with him, the only light this wretched world ever offered. I look upon the velvet shadows of my chamber and see not a place of rest, but a shroud waiting to be claimed. Is it not more beautiful to fade away like a dying rose in the moonlight than to wither slowly in the cruel, grey light of an empty morning? I feel the pull of the great silence, and it calls to me with such a gentle, tragic grace.
